ksa_text: Prepare, validate, and coordinate regulatory data submissions, managing data collection workflows, verification processes, and submission execution while maintaining accuracy documentation and reporting.

dcwf.top_element_label: Continuously validate the organization against policies/guidelines/procedures/regulations/laws to ensure compliance.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Continuously validate the organization against policies/guidelines/procedures/regulations/laws to ensure compliance.
Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of Data Operations. This task KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the Director of Data Operations? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the Director of Data Operations. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of Data Operations. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the Director of Data Operations. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
